title: 10 Best Medicinal Preparations of Apium graveolens (Celery)

preparations.constituent_desc: Apium Graveolens seed oil, also known as celery seed oil, is derived from the seeds of the celery plant through a process of steam distillation. The oil is known for its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic properties, making it a popular natural remedy for conditions such as arthritis, high blood pressure, and poor digestion. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the seeds, which are harvested from the celery plant and then dried before being steam distilled to extract the oil. To make the oil, the dried seeds are placed in a still and heated with water, which releases the oil from the seeds. The oil is then collected and filtered to remove any impurities. When using celery seed oil, it is important to dilute it with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin, as it can cause skin irritation if used undiluted. Additionally, it is not recommended for use during pregnancy, as it may stimulate menstruation and lead to miscarriage. It is also important to note that celery seed oil may interact with certain medications, so it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before using it.

Apium Graveolens juice is a medicinal preparation derived from the celery plant, scientifically known as Apium graveolens. This juice is known for its numerous health benefits, including its ability to reduce inflammation, lower blood pressure, and improve digestion. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the stems and leaves of the celery plant. To make Apium Graveolens juice, simply extract the juice from the celery using a juicer or blender and strain out any solid particles. When using this preparation, it is important to be aware of potential side effects such as allergic reactions or interactions with certain medications. Additionally, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using celery juice as a medicinal preparation, especially for those with pre-existing medical conditions or those who are pregnant or breastfeeding.

Apium Graveolens tincture is a medicinal preparation made by soaking celery plants in alcohol to extract their active compounds. Its main health benefits include reducing inflammation, lowering blood pressure, and providing relief from menstrual cramps. The stalks, leaves, and seeds of the celery plant are used to make this tincture. To prepare it, the plant material is chopped and soaked in alcohol for several weeks before being strained. When using this tincture, it's important to follow the recommended dosage, as high doses can cause skin sensitivity and photosensitivity. Additionally, pregnant women and individuals with low blood pressure should avoid using celery tincture, as it may lower blood pressure further and stimulate uterine contractions.

Apium Graveolens infusion is a medicinal preparation made by steeping the leaves, seeds, or stems of the celery plant in hot water to extract its beneficial compounds. The main health benefits of this infusion include reducing inflammation, providing relief from arthritis and muscle spasms, and supporting digestion and liver function. The leaves, seeds, and stems of the Apium graveolens plant are used to make this preparation. To make the infusion, add 1-2 teaspoons of dried celery leaves or seeds to a cup of boiling water, let it steep for 10-15 minutes, and then strain before drinking. It is important to note that high doses of celery infusion can have diuretic effects and interact with certain medications, so it is recommended to consult with a healthcare provider before using this preparation, especially for those who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have kidney or liver problems.

Apium Graveolens tea is an herbal infusion made from the seeds, leaves, or stems of the celery plant, Apium graveolens. Its main health benefits include reducing inflammation, lowering blood pressure, and providing relief for digestive issues such as bloating and constipation. The seeds, leaves, and stems of the celery plant are used to make this medicinal tea. To prepare the tea, add one to two teaspoons of dried celery seeds or chopped celery leaves to a cup of boiling water, let it steep for 10-15 minutes, then strain and drink. It is important to consult with a healthcare provider before consuming celery tea, especially for those who are pregnant, breastfeeding, or have a history of kidney problems, as it may lower blood pressure and have diuretic effects.

Apium Graveolens essential oil is a concentrated hydrophobic liquid extracted from the seeds, fruits, and leaves of the celery plant through a steam distillation process. The main health benefits of this Apium Graveolens preparation include anti-inflammatory, analgesic, and sedative properties, making it useful for treating conditions such as arthritis, muscular pain, and insomnia. The primary plant parts used for this Apium Graveolens preparation are the seeds and leaves, which contain high levels of active compounds such as terpenes and flavonoids. To make this Apium Graveolens preparation, the plant material is steeped in a carrier oil, such as olive or jojoba oil, and then heated using a double boiler method for several hours. After the plant material is strained out, the resulting liquid is bottled and stored in a cool, dark place. When using this Apium Graveolens preparation, it is important to take several precautions, including diluting the essential oil with a carrier oil before applying it to the skin, performing a patch test to check for skin sensitivity, and avoiding use during pregnancy or while breastfeeding.

Apium Graveolens water extract is a preparation made by extracting the beneficial compounds from the celery plant through a process of boiling and steeping the plant in hot water. Its main health benefits include reducing inflammation, lowering blood pressure, and providing antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the stems, leaves, and seeds of the celery plant. To make the extract, first, clean and chop the celery, then add it to boiling water and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes. After that, strain the liquid and discard the solids. As for precautions, it is important to note that celery extract may cause allergic reactions in some people, and it may interact with certain medications, so it is recommended to consult a healthcare professional before using it. Additionally, it is important to consume it in moderation, as high doses can cause photosensitivity and other side effects.

Apium Graveolens hydrosol is a fragrant, water-based solution derived from the steam distillation of the celery plant (Apium graveolens). Its main health benefits include calming and soothing properties, making it useful for skin irritations, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation. The key plant parts used for this preparation are the leaves, stems, and seeds of the celery plant. To make Apium Graveolens hydrosol, the celery plant is steeped in boiling water, and the resulting steam is collected and condensed. The main precautions to take when using this Apium Graveolens preparation include performing a patch test before widespread use, as some people may experience skin irritation, and avoiding ingestion, as it can have toxic effects.

Apium Graveolens poultice is a medicinal preparation made from the leaves and seeds of the celery plant, Apium graveolens, which are crushed or ground and applied topically to the skin as a paste or compress. The main health benefits of this celery preparation include its anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving properties, making it useful for treating minor injuries, sprains, and inflammation. The leaves and seeds of the celery plant are the main plant parts used for this preparation, which contain various compounds such as flavonoids, essential oils, and antioxidants that contribute to its medicinal properties. To make an Apium Graveolens poultice, first, crush or grind the celery leaves and seeds into a paste, then apply it directly to the affected area and cover it with a cloth or bandage. To avoid any potential skin irritation or allergic reactions, it is important to perform a patch test on a small area of skin before applying the poultice to a larger area. Additionally, it is recommended to consult with a healthcare professional before using celery poultice as a treatment, especially for individuals with known allergies to celery or other related plants, and for those who are pregnant or breastfeeding.

Apium Graveolens rhizome extract is a substance derived from the underground stem of the celery plant, which has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits. This extract is rich in antioxidants, vitamins, and minerals, and has been found to have anti-inflammatory, diuretic, and digestive properties. The main plant parts used for this preparation are the celery root and lower stem, which are harvested, cleaned, and dried before being extracted using a solvent. To make the extract, the dried celery root and stem are chopped and placed in a solvent, such as ethanol or water, which extracts the beneficial compounds. The liquid is then filtered and evaporated to leave behind a concentrated extract. When using Apium Graveolens rhizome extract, it is important to follow the recommended dosage, as excessive use may cause skin irritation, photosensitivity, or interact with certain medications. Additionally, pregnant women and individuals with kidney or liver disease should consult a healthcare professional before using this preparation.

intro: Apium graveolens, also known as celery, is a plant that has been used in medicinal preparations for centuries. Its seeds, leaves, and stalks are used to make tisanes, which are herbal teas, and tinctures. The tisane is made by steeping the celery leaves or seeds in hot water, and it is believed to have diuretic and anti-inflammatory properties. The tincture is made by soaking the celery seeds in alcohol, and it is used to treat various ailments such as rheumatism, arthritis, and neuralgia. Additionally, celery seeds have been used in traditional medicine to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. However, it is important to note that more research is needed to confirm these claims and to establish appropriate dosages. As with any herbal remedy, it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before use.
